mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation expectations that compress real equity returns, generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumption and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen > 0.2pp over the preceding 3 months during any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad-based demand acceleration that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The signal requires both series to move together; either alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% and CPIAUCSL accelerates by more than 0.2% month-over-month, restrictive monetary policy combined with inflation acceleration compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL accelerates > 0.2% month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals contracting industrial activity that reduces earnings forecasts for cyclical sectors, generating negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in HOUST signals accelerating residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, generating positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST increases month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decrease in UNRATE and increase in PAYEMS indicates robust labor market absorption that boosts consumer spending forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. Either variable moving alone fails to capture the full signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS exceeding 0.25 percentage points over a three-month window signals active monetary easing that lowers discount rates and credit spreads, generating positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS has fallen > 0.25pp over the prior 3 months during any 3-year rolling window.
